dialogue.utterance: Unit 3, respond to reported psychiatric problem/suicide attempt at 30th and Broad in [CITY].
Hi, I'm Alex with emergency medical services. What made you call 911 today?
I took a bunch of Coricidin HBP to get high. It's been a while now, and I'm seeing things when I close my eyes.
Thanks for sharing that. Let me just confirm with you - how many pills did you take?
32 pills of Coricidin HBP. I do this sometimes, but today I felt anxious and wanted to get high again. I'm visualizing things when my eyes are closed.
Let's check your alertness. Can you state your name, location, and the date for us?
My name is Mike. I'm at my apartment in [CITY], and the date is November 8th.
Alert and oriented to person, place, and time. Pupils are equal, round, reactive, and measuring 4 mm bilaterally. Eyes are tracking normally.
Eyes are open spontaneously and reacting to light.
You are alert at baseline. Your eye opening is spontaneous.
Verbal responses are oriented. You are answering correctly and consistently.
Motor responses are appropriate. You are following commands and cooperating well.
When did the hallucinations begin after taking the pills?
Shortly after ingestion. Just as they started to take effect.
Do you have any allergies or reactions to medications?
I'm allergic to the pertussis vaccine. No other known allergies.
Are you currently taking any prescriptions or over-the-counter medications?
None, currently.
Would you be willing to share any past medical history or substance use patterns?
I've used heroin, cocaine, and crack in the past. My substance of choice has been DXM. I had Hepatitis C in the past but haven't had medical issues recently. I was clean for a few days before this.
On a scale from 0 to 10, how would you rate your physical pain right now?
Zero - I feel no pain in my body.
Moving to the physical assessment. Let's first check your airway. You're breathing fine, and your airway is open and patent.
Preferred posture to maintain airway is seated upright or semi Fowler's. Airway is patent and not obstructed.
No sounds of airway obstruction present.
Your breathing is steady, regular, and there are no signs of distress. Any unusual breathing sensations?
Breathing is normal and pain-free.
Rate and depth of respirations appear within normal limits at 16 per minute. No cyanosis observed.
Position of the trachea appears centered. Work of breathing seems normal.
No bilateral or asymmetrical chest movement observed.
No flaring of nostrils or signs of accessory muscles being used for breathing.
Bilateral breath sounds are present and equal. Adventitious breath sounds are not heard.
Oxygen saturation measured at 100% on room air.
Let's start by getting your vital signs. Partner, we'll need the blood pressure and pulse first.
Blood pressure is 164 over 110.
Pulse rate is 136 and regular.
Respirations are 16 per minute, normal depth and rhythm.
Oxygen saturation is at 100% on room air.
Blood sugar is 110 mg/dL. Normal.
EKG shows sinus tachycardia without any ectopy.
Regarding the hallucinations you mentioned, are you feeling confused, lightheaded, or have trouble moving properly?
I'm fine - just visual hallucinations when I close my eyes and feeling a bit stimulated, but mentally I'm okay.
HEENT findings are unremarkable. Pupils are PERRL and tracking normally.
I'll listen to your chest next. Please take a deep breath for me.
Okay.
Breath sounds are clear and equal bilaterally. No signs of chest discomfort or tenderness.
Try to lift your arm or grip my finger for me.
Good bilateral strength and coordination noted. No deformity or trauma identified.
Skin is warm, dry, with capillary refill less than 2 seconds. No rashes or significant findings noted on inspection.
Skin is intact with no petechiae, purpura, abrasions, bruises, or scars. No rashes or bite/sting marks.
Skin turgor appears normal. Temperature is within expected range and skin color shows no jaundice or pallor.
Given your detailed history, vitals, and exam findings, we will follow protocol 7.1 for overdose - medical, opioid.
Mike, we're preparing to start an IV and perform some cardiac monitoring for your safety and further assessment.
Sounds good.
IV initiated in the left arm antecubital vein.
Cardiac monitoring now active and indicates sinus tachycardia.
Twelve-lead EKG performed and consistent with baseline.
Let's recheck your vitals now. Pulse?
Pulse is 130 now.
Blood pressure is 146/89.
Oxygen saturation has decreased to 96% and blood sugar is now zero.
We'll assist you into the ambulance in a semi-Fowler's position for comfort and postural stability. Please belt in now.
Mike, we are setting up the four-lead ECG monitor for continuous cardiac monitoring during transport.
[COMPANY_NAME] has been notified of your condition and status. We are coordinating your transport under protocol.
You will be transported to [COMPANY_NAME]. Medical staff are ready for your arrival.

[CITY], this is Unit 3 with a 28 male presenting with DXM overdose. Known history of opioid use disorder. Current vitals: pulse 130, BP 146/89, 96% O2, and glucose is zero. IV is running, EKG shows sinus tach. Hallucinations reported but alert and oriented with GCS 15.
Transport underway, ETA five minutes. Arriving in hall bed 3.
We will maintain contact for handover and updates. Thank you.
GCS remains 15, with Eye = 4, Verbal = 5, and Motor = 5, A/O x 4. State remains stable.
No chest wall tenderness or discoloration noted on inspection.
Gross visual assessment of your head and neck shows no deformities, swellings, or discharges.
Extraocular movements are normal. Neck veins are not distended. No swallowing difficulties or nuchal rigidity.
No signs of lymphadenopathy or neck masses. No scarring noted on the head or neck area.
Examination of the ears, nose, and throat reveal no hemorrhage, drainage, or obstruction.
No foreign bodies in ears or nose. Gross visual assessment of hearing appears intact.
Sunken eyes not observed. Tongue and throat structures appear normal for visual inspection.
Comparison of peripheral and central pulses reveals no discrepancies. No significant heart murmurs or irregular rhythms heard.
Any chest discomfort or palpitations that you can share?
No chest pain. I mainly feel it when I close my eyes and start hallucinating.
Abdomen appears soft and unremarkable in shape and size.
Bowel sounds are present, but not assessed in detail at this time.
No tenderness, rigidity, or guarding found on palpation. No mass appreciated.
No pelvic instability, crepitus, or pain reported in the flank or abdominal area.
No complaints of rectal bleeding or urine color changes.
You are not reporting urgency, frequency of urination, or changes in stool color.
Continue testing motor function by lifting and extending your limbs.
Peripheral pulses remain strong and consistent.
No inability to use extremities. No angulation or deformity observed.
Vertebral column, flank, and buttocks show no signs of trauma or pain on palpation.
4-lead monitoring shows continuous signs of sinus tachycardia. No deterioration observed yet.
We are applying shoulder and lap belts to secure you safely in semi-Fowlers position for transport.
Ambulance is en route and we are coordinating any state resources that may be needed along the way.
Given the slight decrease in oxygen and glucose, we will maintain cardiac monitoring and IV access during transport.
Your pain level remains consistently at zero. Any changes we should know about?
No change - still no physical pain.
Let me confirm a few things again. When was your last oral intake before this episode?
Several hours ago. I haven't eaten or had anything else after the pills.
Please hold still while we move you into the ambulance for continued care and transport.
Transport has been secured with all monitoring and IV preparations in place.
Once at the hospital, you will be placed in hall bed 3 per hospital instructions.
Reiterating: 28 year-old male, presenting with DXM overdose, no pain, vitals trending with pulse 130, BP 146/89, 96% O2, glucose zero.
GCS 15, hallucinations present, currently stable with IV placed in left arm, EKG consistent with sinus tachycardia.
The hospital is standing by. Good luck, Mike, and we'll hand you over shortly.
All transport preparations and documentation have been completed. Smooth ride ahead.
All set. Paramedics and EMTs will handle you with care. Safe arrival tonight.
Ambulance is moving Toward [CITY]. Everything is stable after last vital check.
Journey is estimated to take five minutes. Fast and safe, as categorized.
Update: Your vitals and assessments are being constantly monitored and reported.
No changes in the physical condition since last check. Hallucinations remain controlled.
Pre-Arrival communication is in place with hospital staff. They are ready upon seating.
Address and DOB noted in documentation. Patient transfer has been initiated.
Final check before arrival-everything is in readiness.
Transport complete. You are safely with the hospital medical team now. Well done.
